title: Motion Tracks: A Unified Representation for Human-Robot Transfer in Few-Shot Imitation Learning
title_zh: Motion Tracks：面向少样本模仿学习中人机迁移的统一表示
abstract: Teaching robots to autonomously complete everyday tasks remains a challenge. Imitation Learning (IL) is a powerful approach that imbues robots with skills via demonstrations, but is limited by the labor-intensive process of collecting teleoperated robot data. Human videos offer a scalable alternative, but it remains difficult to directly train IL policies from them due to the lack of robot action labels. To address this, we propose to represent actions as short-horizon 2D trajectories on an image. These actions, or motion tracks, capture the predicted direction of motion for both human hands and robot end-effectors. We instantiate an IL policy called Motion Track Policy (MT- $\pi$ ) which receives image observations and outputs motion tracks as actions. By leveraging this unified, cross-embodiment action space, MT- $\pi$ completes tasks with high success given just minutes of human video and limited additional robot demonstrations. At test time, we predict motion tracks from two camera views, recovering 6DoF trajectories via multi-view synthesis. MT- $\pi$ achieves an average success rate of 86.5% across 4 real-world tasks, outperforming state-of-the-art IL baselines which do not leverage human data or our action space by 40%, and generalizes to scenarios seen only in human videos. Code and videos are available on our website (https://portal-cornell.github.io/motion_track_policy/).
abstract_zh: 教会机器人自主完成日常任务仍是一个挑战。模仿学习（IL）是一种通过演示赋予机器人技能的有效方法，但受限于收集遥操作机器人数据的劳动密集型过程。人类视频提供了一种可扩展的替代方案，但由于缺乏机器人动作标签，直接从中训练IL策略仍然困难。为此，我们提出将动作表示为图像上的短时2D轨迹。这些动作，即运动轨迹（motion tracks），捕捉了人类手部和机器人末端执行器的预测运动方向。我们实例化了一个名为Motion Track Policy (MT-π)的IL策略，它接收图像观测并输出运动轨迹作为动作。通过利用这种统一的跨实体动作空间，MT-π仅需几分钟的人类视频和少量额外的机器人演示即可高成功率完成任务。在测试时，我们从两个相机视角预测运动轨迹，通过多视角合成恢复6DoF轨迹。MT-π在4个真实世界任务中平均成功率达到86.5%，比不使用人类数据或我们动作空间的最先进IL基线高出40%，并且能够泛化到仅在人类视频中见过的场景。代码和视频可在我们的网站上获取（https://portal-cornell.github.io/motion_track_policy/）。
tldr_zh: 提出运动轨迹统一表示，实现从人类视频到机器人的少样本模仿学习。
cover_zh: 机器人看人类视频就能学会新技能？这个统一动作表示让成功率飙升40%
